question: I'm analyzing simple battery assault trends in Los Angeles. First, identify the year between 2010 and 2019 that saw the highest number of these incidents. In that same year, which LAPD area recorded the most arrests for other miscellaneous violations? Focusing on that specific area, how many simple assault battery incidents occurred there in 2020?. Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/crime-data-from-2010-to-2019/files/rows.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

df["DATE OCC"] = pd.to_datetime(df["DATE OCC"], errors="coerce")
answer = (
    df.loc[df["Crm Cd Desc"] == "BATTERY - SIMPLE ASSAULT"]
      .assign(year=df["DATE OCC"].dt.year)
      .loc[lambda x: x["year"].between(2010, 2019, inclusive="both")]
      .groupby("year")
      .size()
      .sort_values(ascending=False)
      .index[0]
)
answer = int(answer)
answer = str(answer)
1.subquestion: From 2010 to 2019, which year has the most Los Angeles incidents with crime description "BATTERY - SIMPLE ASSAULT"?

2.fact:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/arrest-data-from-2010-to-2019/files/rows.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

df["Arrest Date"] = pd.to_datetime(df["Arrest Date"], errors="coerce")
answer = (
    df.assign(year=df["Arrest Date"].dt.year)
      .loc[
          lambda x: (x["year"] == 2016)
          & (x["Charge Group Description"] == "Miscellaneous Other Violations")
      ]
      .groupby("Area Name")
      .size()
      .sort_values(ascending=False)
      .index[0]
)
2.subquestion: In <node_1 answer> Los Angeles arrest records, which area has the most arrests in the charge group "Miscellaneous Other Violations"?

3.fact:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/lapd-ripa-ab-953-stop-incident-details-from-7-1-2018-to-present/files/rows.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

area_id_value = (
    df.loc[df["Area Name"] == "Central", "Area ID"]
      .dropna()
      .iloc[0]
)
answer = int(str(area_id_value).split("/")[0])
answer = str(answer)
3.subquestion: Using LAPD stop-incident records collected under California's RIPA program, what numeric Area ID corresponds to area name <node_2 answer> (answer as an integer)?

5.fact:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/crime-data-from-2020-to-present/files/rows.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

df["DATE OCC"] = pd.to_datetime(df["DATE OCC"], errors="coerce")
answer = int(
    df.assign(year=df["DATE OCC"].dt.year)
      .loc[
          lambda x: (x["year"] == 2020)
          & (x["AREA"] == 1)
          & (x["Crm Cd"] == 624)
      ]
      .shape[0]
)
answer = str(answer)
5.subquestion: Using the 2020 to present Los Angeles crime records, in 2020 how many incidents with crime code <node_4 answer> occurred in Area ID <node_3 answer>?

reasoning_chain: Node 1: In 2010 to 2019 LA crime records, the year with the most BATTERY - SIMPLE ASSAULT incidents -> 2016.
Node 2 depends on node 1: In 2016 arrests, the area with the most Miscellaneous Other Violations arrests -> Central.
Node 3 depends on node 2: In LAPD RIPA stop-incident records, Central corresponds to Area ID 1.
Node 4: In Los Angeles crime records, BATTERY - SIMPLE ASSAULT corresponds to crime code 624.
Node 5 depends on nodes 3-4: In 2020 crime records for Area ID 1, incident count where Crm Cd = 624 -> 1350.
